title: Bound async task lifetimes and honor cancellation
description: Tie spawned tasks to a parent scope and propagate cancellation so no task outlives or ignores its caller.
body: Use when spawning async tasks, awaiting with timeouts, or handling shutdown. Do not fire-and-forget tasks that keep running after the request is cancelled, and do not swallow a cancellation signal in a long loop. Actions: use structured concurrency (a scope that joins/cancels its children), check the cancellation token at await points and loop iterations, and release resources on cancel via finally/defer. Validation signals include a cancelled parent leaving no running children and a timeout actually stopping the work. Risk boundary: a task that continues mutating state after cancellation can double-apply effects; make cancellation cooperative and verify no work proceeds past it.
